title_en: You’ve Never Heard of China’s Greatest Sci-Fi Novel
title_zh: 你从未听说过中国最伟大的科幻小说

paragraphs.en: Ma Qianzhu was unsatisfied with Chinese progress. An engineer at a large state-owned enterprise, he belonged to a generation that grew up believing engineering is destiny, that China’s future would be built, bolt by bolt, by people like him. Then Ma discovered something extraordinary: a wormhole to the late Ming Dynasty. With more than 500 peers, he commandeered a ship and traveled back in time 400 years, to a preindustrial China wracked by foreign invasion and internal decay. Their mission: trigger an industrial revolution in the past that would, in the future, make modern China great (again).
paragraphs.zh: 马乾柱对中国的进步不满。作为一家大型国有企业的工程师，他属于那一代人：这一代人相信工程是命运，相信中国的未来会由像他这样的人，一颗颗螺栓、一根根钢轨地修建起来。后来，马发现了一件非凡之事：一条通往明末的虫洞。凭借五百多名同伴，他劫持了一艘船，回到了四百年前，返回到一个被外敌入侵与内乱衰败所重创的前工业化中国。他们的任务是：在过去引发一场工业革命，以便在未来让现代中国再次伟大。

paragraphs.en: Here are 23 ways China is rewiring the future.
paragraphs.zh: 以下是中国正在重塑未来的23种方式。

paragraphs.en: This, strictly speaking, did not happen. It’s the plot of The Morning Star of Lingao (临高启明), a sprawling, collectively written science-fiction web novel that has consumed a corner of the Chinese internet for nearly two decades. It now totals millions of words. It has never been translated into English. Almost no one in the West knows it exists.
paragraphs.zh: 严格说来，这件事并未真实发生。它是《临高启明》的情节——这部规模宏大的群体创作科幻网络小说已经在中国互联网角落里占据近二十年，现有内容已达数百万字。它从未被译成英文。西方几乎无人知道它的存在。

paragraphs.en: But I would argue—somewhat less strictly speaking—that the events of the book did happen. That the time travel worked. And that the secret to understanding modern China is all right there, contained within its prophetic, often frightening pages.
paragraphs.zh: 但我想说——稍微放松一点——书中的事件确实发生过。可以说，时空穿越是有效的。理解当代中国的关键，就完全藏在它那预言性、常常令人毛骨悚然的文字里。

paragraphs.en: In 2006, a post went up on SC BBS, China’s earliest military-themed message board: What would you do if you could travel back to the Ming Dynasty with modern knowledge? The question struck a nerve. The Ming Dynasty occupies a painful place in Chinese historical consciousness. It was a period when Chinese civilization entered a long decline, ending in the so-called Great Divergence. Europe embraced discovery, ascent, and construction; China sealed itself off. Joseph Needham’s famous question—“Why did modern science develop in Europe but not in China?”—has haunted modern Chinese intellectual life ever since.
paragraphs.zh: 2006年，中国最早的军事主题论坛SC BBS上出现了一则帖子：如果你能带着现代知识穿越回明朝，你会怎么做？这个问题触动了许多人。明朝在中国人的历史记忆里占据着一个敏感而痛苦的位置。那是中华文明进入长期衰落并最终走向所谓“伟大分化”的阶段。欧洲走向了发现、上升与建造；中国则把自己封闭了起来。自那以后，约瑟夫·尼达姆那句著名的问题——“为什么近代科学在欧洲发展了，而在中国没有？”——一直困扰着近代中国人的思想世界。

paragraphs.en: The Morning Star of Lingao emerged as a kind of internet-fueled continuation of this historic discourse. As more people found the original post, forum discussions crystallized into serious, collective story-writing. If you could travel back to the Ming Dynasty with modern knowledge, these users decided, well, you’d obviously industrialize before Europe and win modernity.
paragraphs.zh: 《灵稿晨星》正是在这种历史论辩中，凭借网络力量出现并延续了下去。随着更多人看到最初的帖子，论坛讨论逐渐凝聚成一场严肃的集体写作。如果你能带着现代知识回到明朝，这些用户便下了结论：显然你可以在欧洲之前完成工业化，赢得现代性。

paragraphs.en: Not everyone quite saw it that way. This was also the moment when China’s internet began producing its first generation of liberal-minded intellectuals, who debated everything in a relatively free online space—air pollution, labor rights, the brutal relocations preceding Beijing’s 2008 Olympic spectacle. I was a teenager coming of age in this internet. I bore witness to the Arab Spring and its Chinese-inspired “Jasmine Revolution.” I devoured Charter 08, a manifesto for political reform and human rights. In 2011, while I was in high school, the renowned blogger Han Han released his pro-democracy trilogy—“On Revolution,” “On Democracy,” “On Freedom.”
paragraphs.zh: 并非所有人都这样想。与此同时，中国互联网也开始孕育出第一代较为自由开明的网络知识分子，大家在相对开放的线上空间里讨论一切——空气污染、劳动权益、北京2008年奥运会盛会前的强制搬迁。那是我成长中的网络时代。曾目睹阿拉伯之春以及其在中国引发的“茉莉花革命”。我通读了《08宪章》，那是一份倡导政治改革与人权的宣言。2011年，我还在高中时，知名博主韩寒发布了他的反体制三部曲——《在关于革命》、《在关于民主》和《在关于自由》。

paragraphs.en: The Lingao writers had little interest in these values. If the novel didn’t exactly inspire China’s effort to industrialize, it certainly reflected and reinforced it, serving as a kind of sacred text. In 2011, a nationalist economist named Wang Xiaodong coined the term “Industrial Party”—not a literal party (there’s effectively only one in China) but a way to refer to a circle of internet thinkers and influencers writing essays and cheering on plans the state had initiated. As the scholar Li Qiang has observed, the Industrial Party emphasized a pragmatic attitude of “shelving disputes and focusing on development.” The force they celebrated, ostensibly derived from industrial civilization, was actually development itself—progress as ideology, building as salvation. Revolution, democracy, freedom: These were distractions, even obstacles. What mattered was building. What mattered was speed.
paragraphs.zh: 而《灵稿晨星》的作者们对这些价值并不感兴趣。若说这部小说并未真正推动中国的工业化进程，它至少映照并强化了这一进程，成为一种近乎神圣的文本。2011年，民族主义经济学家王晓东提出了“工业党”一词。这并非真正意义上的政党（中国事实上只有一个执政党），而是指一群网络上的思想者和意见领袖，他们撰写文章，助推并歌颂国家启动的一系列发展方案。正如学者李强所观察到的，工业党强调一种务实态度：把争议暂时搁置，集中精力发展。表面上看，他们颂扬的力量来自工业文明，但实际崇拜的是“发展”本身——把进步当成意识形态，把建设当成拯救。革命、民主、自由：这些在他们看来都是分散注意力的，甚至是阻碍。重要的是建设。重要的是速度。

paragraphs.en: And perhaps no figure better embodied the Industrial Party’s evolution from internet subculture to influential public voice than the man who inspired the character of Ma Qianzhu—Ma Qianzu.
paragraphs.zh: 而若要找一个人最能体现“工业党”从网络亚文化走向有影响力的公共声音的转变，那就是激发马浅舟形象的那个人——马谦祖。

paragraphs.en: That’s right: the fictional engineer in Lingao is based on a real-life engineer of the same name. (Or nearly: Their names differ by two Chinese characters.) The writing process for the book was extremely decentralized, so attribution is often messy, but there’s a consensus that the real-life Ma was one of the book’s writers. Ma—whose real name is actually Ren Chonghao, but he goes by his pen name—trained as a civil engineer and worked at the Suzhou Planning & Design Research Institute. He poured his own dreams and convictions into his fictional counterpart.
paragraphs.zh: 没错：灵高中的那位虚构工程师，原型来自一位现实中的工程师，名字也差不多相同——两者只差两个中文字符。该书的写作过程高度去中心化，署名关系常常非常混乱，但普遍共识是，现实中的马确实是这本书的作者之一。这个马的真名其实是任重浩，他使用笔名。他受过土木工程训练，曾在苏州规划与设计研究院工作，并把自己的梦想与信念都注入了书中的虚构人物。

paragraphs.en: Ma’s moment arrived in 2011. On July 23, two high-speed trains collided near Wenzhou, killing 40 people. The accident traumatized the nation for what it seemed to reveal about the costs of China’s breakneck pace of development. A prominent essay captured the mood, its title becoming a rallying cry: “China, Slow Down, Wait for Your People.” The prose read almost like prayer: “China, please stop your flying pace, wait for your people, wait for your soul, wait for your morality, wait for your conscience!”
paragraphs.zh: 马在2011年迎来了关键时刻。2011年7月23日，距离温州不远两列高铁相撞，造成40人遇难。这起事故让全国心有余悸，因为它似乎揭露了中国高速发展所付出的代价。一篇颇具影响力的随笔抓住了当时的舆论情绪，标题成为了口号：“中国，要放慢脚步，先等人。”文中措辞几乎像祈祷：“中国，请你慢下你的飞速，请你等你的人，请你等你灵魂的归宿，请你等你的道德良知，请你等你的良知与良心！”

paragraphs.en: Ma and other Industrial Party voices responded with a counteroffensive. The solution wasn’t to slow down but to double down, they said—to learn from mistakes, to push through the difficult phase when new technologies were still being mastered. And key to their campaign was Lingao itself. The writing of it became a phenomenon across Chinese internet forums in the 2010s: Its open source ethos and collaborative methods deeply appealed to China’s burgeoning tech community. Beyond regular meetups among core contributors, Lingao’s creation fostered the formation of China’s “keyboard politics”—online communities where users engage in fierce debates about governance, policy, and national direction under the protection of pseudonyms. These conversations became staging grounds for political arguments that couldn’t happen elsewhere, where amateur policy wonks, military enthusiasts, and armchair strategists honed their worldviews. In 2012, the nationalist commentary website Guancha (think of it as China’s Breitbart) was founded, and its complex entanglement with Industrial Party thought and personnel networks demonstrated how far Lingao’s influence extended beyond mere time-travel fiction.
paragraphs.zh: 马与其他“工业党”人士的回应，是发起反击。他们说，出路不是放慢，而是加倍推进——从错误中吸取教训，在新技术仍在被掌握的艰难阶段继续前行。与此同时，灵高本身成了他们行动的关键。其写作在2010年代成为中文互联网论坛上的现象：它的开源理念和协作方式，深深吸引了中国日益壮大的技术社区。除去核心作者之间的定期聚会外，灵高的创作还催生了中国的“键盘政治”——在网名保护下，用户在线激烈辩论治理、政策和国家走向的社区。这些讨论成了政治争论的练兵场，在那里业余政策研究者、军迷和沙发军事战略家打磨着自己的世界观。2012年，民族主义评论网站“观察者网”（可视作中国的《布雷特巴特》）成立，而它与“工业党”思想与人员网络的复杂纠葛，显示出灵高的影响力早已超越了单纯的“穿越小说”边界。

paragraphs.en: Industrial Party ideology ends up being quite Darwinian. What matters most is the power that flows from industrial capacity. This contributes to what scholars refer to as the party’s “aesthetic.” Fred Gao, a Beijing-based journalist who identifies with the Industrial Party and who briefly worked for Guancha, told me: “These people view industrialization as the highest form of beauty. Building things from nothing—that’s their romanticism.”
paragraphs.zh: 工业党的意识形态最终变得颇具达尔文主义意味。最重要的，是从工业能力中流出的力量。这也造就了学者们所说的该党“审美观”。来自北京、认同工业党的记者高飞（Fred Gao）——他曾短暂在《观察者网》（Guancha）任职——告诉我：“这些人把工业化看作最高级的美。能从无到有创造出事物——这就是他们的浪漫主义。”

paragraphs.en: Of course, the techno-nationalist impulse transcends borders. “Elon Musk is the ultimate Industrial Party figure,” Gao said. Musk’s vision of colonizing Mars, his impatience with regulation, his worship of engineering solutions, his conviction that making physical things matters more than anything else—his “aesthetic has strong resonances with the Chinese Industrial Party,” Gao said. What differs is simply the political system that channels it.
paragraphs.zh: 当然，这种技术民族主义的冲动早已超越国界。“伊隆·马斯克是最典型的工业党人物，”高飞说。马斯克关于殖民火星的愿景、对监管的不耐、对工程解决方案的崇拜、以及他那种“制造有形物品比其他任何事都更重要”的信念——高飞说，“他的审美观与中国工业党有着强烈共鸣。”不同之处仅在于将其引导出来的政治制度不同。

paragraphs.en: Almost nobody, including me, can finish all the chapters of Lingao (to say nothing of the more than 1,400 derivative works). It’s not just that it’s too long. Reading it is quite painful. The novel’s language and narrative structure are aggressively anti-literary. To write beautifully would be bourgeois, the Industrial Party seemed to believe. Technical descriptions veer into what feels like self-indulgence, an uncomfortable disregard for readers without STEM backgrounds.
paragraphs.zh: 几乎没有人（包括我）能读完《臧洛》全部章节，更别说那超过1,400部衍生作品了。不仅仅因为太长。阅读本身也相当痛苦。该小说的语言和叙事结构都带有强烈的反文学色彩。对这个党派而言，写得漂亮似乎是资产阶级的，而技术性描写则常常滑向一种近乎自我放纵的状态，对没有理工科背景的读者缺乏照顾，令人不适。

paragraphs.en: The book asks questions like: How do you solve energy problems when you can’t drill for oil? How do you begin mechanization without the machine tools to make machine tools? How do you produce nitric acid when you’re starting from literally dirt? From Chapter 22, as the 500 time travelers plan their expedition to colonize Lingao County in Hainan (the southern island in China):
paragraphs.zh: 这本书提出了这样的疑问：当你无法钻井取油时，如何解决能源问题？当你没有制造机床的机床时，如何开始机械化？当你起点几乎是赤手空拳、只有泥土时，如何生产硝酸？在第22章，500名时空旅者计划前往海南（中国南部海岛）临高县开辟殖民地：

paragraphs.en: “Besides complete equipment sets, we need backup chemical engineering equipment: synthesis towers, absorption towers, decarburization towers, distillation columns, saturated hot water towers, various reaction kettles, pressure-resistant piping, acid pumps, heaters... I estimate we need 500–600 tons of materials.”
paragraphs.zh: “除了完整的设备套装，我们还需要备用的化工设备：合成塔、吸收塔、脱碳塔、蒸馏塔、饱和热水塔、各类反应釜、耐压管道、酸泵、加热器……我估计至少需要五百到六百吨材料。”

paragraphs.en: “Can’t we produce these ourselves? Our mechanical industry is fairly complete.”
paragraphs.zh: “我们自己不能制造这些吗？我们的机械工业已经比较完整了。”

paragraphs.en: “Material problems,” Ji Situi said. “Unless we can quickly produce stainless steel and polyethylene, domestic production of chemical equipment will be difficult within 10 years.”
paragraphs.zh: “材料问题，”纪斯推说，“除非我们能很快生产不锈钢和聚乙烯，否则在未来十年内发展化工设备的国产化生产会非常困难。”

paragraphs.en: It reminds me of Daniel Defoe’s Robinson Crusoe, published in 1719. In the story, Crusoe reconstructs the distant island’s physicality from first principles. He builds tools, domesticates animals, plants crops, constructs shelter, makes pottery, bakes bread, sews clothes. The novel works like an instruction manual because, just like Lingao, that is the point. Robinson Crusoe is among the first works of fiction to treat industriousness itself as heroic.
paragraphs.zh: 这让我想起丹尼尔·笛福于1719年发表的《鲁滨逊漂流记》。在故事里，鲁滨逊从最基本的原理出发，重建了这座荒岛的物理世界。他制造工具，驯养动物，种植作物，搭建住所，做陶器，烤面包，缝制衣物。这部小说之所以像一本使用说明书，正因为它和林高一样，都是以此为核心目的。鲁滨逊是最早把“勤勉本身”当作英雄主义来书写的小说之一。”

paragraphs.en: Yet Lingao gets at something more specific than Defoe’s Enlightenment universalism: It reflects the moral worldview of China during its 21st-century industrial acceleration and nationalism. Consider this passage—the scene when the crew has just time-traveled after 47 chapters of preparation:
paragraphs.zh: 不过，《Lingao》触及到的内容比笛福的启蒙普遍主义更具体：它反映了中国在二十一世纪工业加速与民族主义高涨时期的道德世界观。请看这一段——这是船员在经过47章的准备后刚刚完成时空穿越时的场景：

paragraphs.en: Behind them, a crimson sun erupts from the horizon, painting the sky red. The South China Sea’s surface glitters with golden light as the dawn of a new world illuminates the time travelers for the first time. On deck, they rise involuntarily, overwhelmed by emotion:
paragraphs.zh: 在他们身后，一轮绯红的太阳从地平线上爆发，映得天空通红。南海的海面在金色晨光中闪烁，彷佛新世界的黎明第一次照亮了这群时空旅人。船上，他们不由自主地站了起来，情绪涌上心头：

paragraphs.en: I am witnessing history. I am creating history.
paragraphs.zh: 我正在见证历史。我正在创造历史。

paragraphs.en: Intoxicated by this feeling, someone begins singing “Ode to the Motherland”:
paragraphs.zh: 在这种激动情绪下，有人开始唱起《Ode to the Motherland》（《祖国颂》）：

paragraphs.en: The five-starred red flag flutters in the wind, Victory songs ring so clear, Wesing of our beloved motherland,
paragraphs.zh: 五星红旗在风中飘扬，胜利的歌声格外清亮，颂唱我们心爱的祖国。

paragraphs.en: From today walking toward prosperity and strength …
paragraphs.zh: 从今天起，向着繁荣与强盛前进……

paragraphs.en: First one voice, then many, then all. The soaring song pierces the clouds, resounding across the new world.
paragraphs.zh: 先是一人开口，又是多人和唱，继而汇为一片。那支高昂的歌声穿透云层，在崭新的世界里回荡。

paragraphs.en: This is the emotional core of Lingao—and of the Industrial Party worldview. The novel never separates technological progress from nationalist revival. Building blast furnaces is patriotism. Engineering is loving your country. The characters in the novel are intoxicated by their mission, and the readers are intoxicated by the fantasy. And perhaps most importantly, the writers are intoxicated by the act of collective world-building itself—the collaborative construction of an alternate Chinese modernity.
paragraphs.zh: 这正是《林高》的情感核心，也是“工业党”世界观的核心。小说从不将科技进步与民族复兴分离开来。建造高炉就是爱国，工程学就是爱国。小说中的人物因使命感而沉醉，读者也因这场幻想而沉醉。更重要的是，作者们也同样醉心于“集体世界建构”这一行为本身——即共同搭建一个另一个中国现代性的想象世界。

paragraphs.en: Between 2000 and the mid-2020s, China’s manufacturing output exploded nearly eightfold. The country became “the world’s factory” not gradually but suddenly, and a feedback loop emerged: The Industrial Party worldview and China’s actual industrialization reinforced each other. Meanwhile, Lingao provided a narrative framework that made China’s development inevitable and righteous.
paragraphs.zh: 从2000年到2020年代中期，中国的制造业产出几乎扩大了八倍。这个国家没有是慢慢成为“世界工厂”，而是骤然成为“世界工厂”；由此形成了一个反馈循环：工业党的世界观与中国现实中的工业化进程相互强化。与此同时，Lingao提供了一套叙事框架，使中国的发展显得不可避免且正当。

paragraphs.en: For educated urbanites, especially those with technical backgrounds, it felt like inhabiting science fiction. The Lingao contributors and readers—typically 20- to 40-year-old males in cities, many with STEM careers—were both witnesses to and agents of this transformation. They worked in the factories, the construction firms, the engineering offices. They experienced firsthand what concentrated state resources and marshaled technical expertise could achieve.
paragraphs.zh: 对受过高等教育的城市人，尤其是拥有技术背景的人来说，这种变化像是在生活中的一部科幻小说。Lingao的作者和读者——通常是城市里的20到40岁男性，其中很多从事STEM领域——既是见证者，也是这场转型的参与者。他们在工厂、建筑公司、工程事务所工作，亲眼见证了集中动用国家资源并调动技术专长所能带来的成就。

paragraphs.en: As Dan Wang argues in his book Breakneck, China has become the quintessential “engineering state.” When Xi Jinping stacks his government with aerospace and defense executives, when he demands manufacturing remain at 28 percent of GDP, when he boasts that China produces something in all UN industrial categories—he’s speaking the Industrial Party gospel. “Productive forces are what fundamentally drive the development of human society,” he said in a May 2024 speech, “and are the final cause of all social and political changes.” Everything else—democracy, rights, culture—is derivative, subordinate, ultimately irrelevant compared to the question of whether you can manufacture advanced semiconductors or build hypersonic missiles.
paragraphs.zh: 正如丹·王在他的著作《Breakneck》中所说，中国已经成为一种典型的“工程国家”。当习近平在政府中安排大量航空航天和国防高管，当他要求制造业始终保持在GDP中的28%，当他自豪地宣称中国在联合国所有工业类别里都有东西可产——他都在传达工业党的教义。王在2024年5月的一次讲话中说：“生产力是推动人类社会发展的根本力量，也是所有社会和政治变化的最终原因。”除此之外——无论是民主、权利还是文化——都只是衍生、从属，最终都不及一个问题重要：你是否能制造先进半导体，或打造高超音速导弹。

paragraphs.en: But that first generation of liberal-minded intellectuals—the ones I grew up idolizing—hasn’t given up. In fact, in recent years, cracks have appeared in the Industrial Party’s armor. “Look at Ma Qianzu now,” says Fred Gao. “These past two years, he still talks about industrial systems, but increasingly he’s discussing socialized childcare, how local governments should spend money, local debt problems, reflections on unlimited borrowing. The pure Industrial Party worldview—that building solves everything—can’t address the structural problems China faces now.”
paragraphs.zh: 但那一代我曾崇拜的自由派知识分子并未放弃。事实上，这些年，工业党的铠甲已经出现裂痕。Fred Gao说：“看看马千祖现在吧。过去两年里，他仍在谈工业体系，但越来越多地讨论社会化托幼、地方政府该怎样花钱、地方债务问题，以及对无限制举债的反思。单一的工业党世界观——认为建设本身能解决一切——已经无法回答中国当下面临的结构性问题。”

paragraphs.en: It’s not just Ma. One of the original Lingao authors, writing under the pen name “The Boaster,” has largely withdrawn from public discourse. Those close to the project say he’s focused on his day job, occasionally updating the novel but no longer engaging in the debates that once defined the Industrial Party circle. Even Wang, the economist who coined the name, has moderated his views. In recent years, he has said that China is not yet ready to sever ties with the West—a striking retreat from his earlier confrontational nationalism.
paragraphs.zh: 这不仅仅是马的问题。早期《林高》作者之一，署名“吹牛者”，已经基本从公共讨论中退了出来。接近这一项目的人士表示，他把主要精力放在了本职工作上，偶尔会更新小说，但已不再参与曾经定义“工业党”圈层的争论。甚至连那个创造“工业党”一词的经济学家王，也已经缓和了自己的观点。近几年，他表示中国还没有准备好与西方切断关系——这是他早先那种对立式民族主义立场中一个显著的后退。

paragraphs.en: For many, “the Chinese Dream” has shattered. Birth rates have plummeted. Unemployment among youth has reached staggering levels. Can the “building more” spirit of Lingao still solve problems? I see doubt everywhere. You can’t engineer your way out of a crisis of meaning when an entire generation of young people has chosen to—as the Chinese idiom goes—“lie flat,” rejecting the promise that endless work leads to prosperity.
paragraphs.zh: 对许多人来说，“中国梦”已经破碎。出生率大幅下滑。青年失业率已升至惊人的水平。“林高”所代表的“多建一点”精神，是否还能继续解决问题？我到处都看到怀疑。你无法通过“工业化”手段逃脱意义危机，当今一代年轻人已经选择——正如中文网络流行语所说——“躺平”，他们在拒绝那个“越努力工作就越能致富”的承诺。

paragraphs.en: In a sense, China’s Industrial Party has lost its innocence. Just as in the endless Lingao, the boundless worship of industrialization has given way to more tangled narratives. In one of the novel’s endings (there are many), the time travelers finally succeed in founding their nation. “All the time travelers gathered—a rare occasion—in the Transmigration Palace, a replica of the Soviet Palace designed by Ma Qianzhu himself... The banquet tables overflowed with unimaginably exquisite foods... rare wines of every kind... truly a scene of indulgent excess and intoxication.” The dystopian absurdity reveals a degree of self-awareness: that power, taken to its extreme, leads to corruption and despotism; that if history can be rewritten, the real challenge comes after victory.
paragraphs.zh: 在某种意义上，中国的“工业党”已经失去了纯真。就像那部无休止的《林高》一样，对工业化的无边界崇拜已经让位于更为复杂的叙事。在小说的一个结局中（这部作品有很多结局），时空旅人终于成功建立起了自己的国家。“所有时空旅人都聚集了一齐——这是罕见的一次——在轮回殿中，那是一座由马谦铸本人设计、复制自苏联宫殿的建筑。盛宴长桌上摆满了难以想象的精致食物……各种稀有葡萄酒……真是一个放纵奢靡与沉醉的场景。”这种反乌托邦式的荒诞揭示出一种自我反省：权力走向极致，最终只会导致腐败和专制；历史即使能被重写，真正的考验在于胜利之后。

paragraphs.zh: 你怎么看？欢迎在下方评论区告诉我们你对本文的看法。或者，你也可以向主编写信，[email protected]。
